steps.title: Identify the goal: emphasize a difference between the two compositions
steps.content: The notes show two distinct Native elements: Live On, Heart of My Nation features a Choctaw hymn, while Fantasy Aborigine No. 3 incorporates a Tewa seashell rattle. To "emphasize a difference," a choice must attach each specific element to its own composition, making the contrast explicit and parallel.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — *Live On, Heart of My Nation* and *Fantasy Aborigine No. 3* are two compositions written by Ballard, a classical music composer.: This merely identifies both pieces as Ballard’s work; it states no difference between them. ✗
  • Choice B — In his two compositions *Live On, Heart of My Nation* and *Fantasy Aborigine No. 3*, Ballard blends elements of various Native musical traditions, such as Indigenous hymns or instruments, with Western classical music.: This mentions both element types but does not link each one to a specific composition, so no contrast is drawn. ✗
  • Choice C — While both compositions integrate various Native musical traditions, *Live On, Heart of My Nation* does so by featuring a Choctaw hymn and *Fantasy Aborigine No. 3* does so by incorporating a Tewa seashell rattle.: Parallel structure explicitly maps each Native element to its composition, directly emphasizing the difference. ✓
  • Choice D — Ballard has different approaches to blending Western classical music with elements of various Native musical traditions, such as using Indigenous hymns and instruments in his compositions.: This vaguely alludes to different approaches without demonstrating what those differences actually are. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
